steps.description: Type option 1 to select DB2 system catalog on the DB2 Admin main menu
Press Enter to navigate to the System Catalog Objects screen
Type option D to select Databases on the System Catalog Objects screen
Type a name filter pattern in the Schema field to locate the target database
Press Enter to submit the filter and display the matching Databases list
Type the A line command in the Select field next to the target database to view its authorizations
Press Enter to open the Database Authorizations panel listing all grantees for the selected database
Type the R line command in the Sel field next to the grantee row whose privileges are to be revoked
Press Enter to open the Revoke Database Privileges panel pre-populated with the grantee's current privileges, database name, and From user
Type any character in front of each privilege to mark it for revocation; leave unmarked privileges unchanged
Type the user ID of the grantor performing the revoke in the By field
Press Enter to generate the Revoke Impact Report (Report Revoke Impacts defaults to YES)
Review the Revoke Impact Report showing affected grantees and privileges, then press Enter to page through the report if needed
Press PF3 to return to the Revoke Database Privileges panel
Type NO in the Report Revoke Impacts field so the next Enter executes the REVOKE statement rather than regenerating the impact report
Press Enter to submit the REVOKE statement; a Change Management prompt will appear
Type NO to decline Change Management tracking for this REVOKE statement
Press Enter to confirm and execute the REVOKE statement; the panel will display REVOKE stmt executed on return
Press PF3 to return to the Database Authorizations panel and confirm the revoked grantee entry is no longer listed
